questions.question_text: A sum of money amounts to ₹19,360 in 3 years at 10% per annum, compounded annually. The compound interest (in ₹) earned is (Round off your answer to the nearest paise).

Three automatic doors in a metro station undergo system checks at regular intervals of 14 minutes, 18 minutes, and 21 minutes, respectively. If all three doors are checked together at the start, after how many minutes will they be checked together again for the first time?
Aman and Raj invested ₹12,000 and ₹18,000, respectively, in a business. After one year, they earned a profit of ₹15,000. How much profit will each get?

Two trains, X and Y, start at the same time from Delhi and Mumbai towards each other. After meeting, train X takes 6 hours to reach Mumbai, and train Y takes 8 hours to reach Delhi. If train X's speed is 60 km/h, what is the speed of train Y?
The value of $(\cos^2 67^\circ - \sin^2 23^\circ) + 4 \cos 30^\circ$ is:
Find the median of the following data (correct to two places of decimal).Class Interval 0-6 6-12 12-18 18-24 24-30 30-36Frequency 5 7 10 13 9 8
A cylindrical tank has a radius of 7 m and a height of 11 m. How many litres of water can it hold? (Use $\pi = 22/7$)
A shopkeeper bought 7 decorative lamps for ₹2,450. Due to damage, 2 lamps could not be sold. He sold the remaining lamps at ₹360 each. What is his total loss (in ₹)?
The cost of a certain fabric is directly proportional to its length for a given width. If the cost of 8 meters of the fabric is ₹3,200, what will be the cost of 25 meters of the same fabric?
Find the mode of the following distribution:Class 5-15 15-25 25-35 35-45Frequency 6 14 18 12

40 persons begin to work together on a job. But after some days 10 persons left the work. If the work, that would have been completed in 40 days, completed in 50 days. How many days after the commencement of the work did the 10 persons leave?
If p is a prime number and GCD $(p^2-1, p^2+1) = 2$, then the value of p must be:
A car covers a distance of 180 km in 3 hours. Find its average speed.

Riya and Neha have certain amount of money. If Riya gives ₹20 to Neha, then Riya will have three times as much money as Neha. However, if Neha gives ₹40 to Riya, then Riya will have five times as much money as Neha. What is the total amount of money (in ₹) that Riya and Neha initially had together?

In a fitness park, a straight walking path is divided into three consecutive segments. The length of the first and third segments are 18 m and 90 m respectively. If the length of the middle segment is the mean proportional between the other two, what is its length?
A solid right circular cylinder has curved surface area $1408\pi$ $cm^2$. If the volume of the cylinder is maximum, then the ratio of radius to height is
In a parallelogram, the difference of two adjacent angles is $40^\circ$. Find the measure of the smaller angle.

Two taps can fill a cistern in 4 hours and 18 hours, respectively. A third tap can empty it in 6 hours. How long (in hours) will it take to fill one-fourth of the empty cistern if all the taps are opened together?

The price of an article increases and decreases alternately by 20% every year. If the difference between the price at the end of the second year and third year is ₹1,920, find 40% of the price (in ₹) of the article at the end of the first year.

A shopkeeper offers the following schemes on toys of the same marked price.
(A) Successive discounts of 20% and 30% on any number of toys bought.
(B) Successive discounts of 20%, 4% and 4% on any number of toys bought.
(C) 25% discount on the first 8 toys and 31% discount on each toy thereon.
(D) 7 toys free of cost on buying 8 toys.
A customer wants to buy 8 toys. Which of the above schemes is the least beneficial to him?
Simplify: $18 - [6 \times 2 - (3 + 1)] \div 7$
The price of an article is decreased by 24%, but the daily sale of the article is increased by 33%. Find the net effect on the daily sale receipts.
If 9 notebooks cost ₹135, how much will 20 notebooks of the same type cost?
Three numbers are in the ratio 5 : 14 : 9, and their LCM is 7560. Their HCF is:
A trader sells rice, claiming 1 kg but actually gives x grams. He marks the price 20% above the cost price and gives a 10% discount on the marked price. If his overall profit is 8%, find x.

Find the value of m which satisfies
$\left(\frac{23}{5}\right)^{13} \times \left(\frac{5}{23}\right)^9 \times \left(\frac{23}{5}\right)^5 = \left(\frac{5}{23}\right)^{6m + 9}$
A shopkeeper sells a watch for ₹840 after giving a 30% discount. What was the marked price (in ₹) of the watch?
Five years ago, the age of Kavita was one-third of the present age of her mother. After 5 years, the sum of their ages will be 75 years. What is Kavita's present age?

Sarang invested a certain sum of money at 10% per annum. After 2 years, he received ₹178.5 as compound interest, compounded annually. Find the corresponding simple interest (in ₹) for 2 years at the same rate.
The sum of two positive numbers is 77 and their product is 1392. The positive difference between them is:
The CPU retrieves the upcoming instruction for execution from which system component?
Who among the following succeeded Raja Ram Mohan Roy as the leader of the Brahmo Samaj?
The sports event Bastar Olympics 2025 was launched in October 2023 in which Indian State?

options.text: It remains the same, as the speed is constant in a given medium
It decreases rapidly because the air molecules move extremely slower
It decreases, because the air becomes less dense
It increases, because the air molecules move faster

questions.question_text: What happens to the speed of sound as the temperature of the air increases?
The Hornbill Festival, celebrated to showcase tribal culture, music, dance, and sports, is associated with which Indian state?
Which river rises west of the Rohtang pass in the Kullu hills of Himachal Pradesh and flows through the Chamba valley of the state?
Which trophy is contested between the England and Scotland teams in the Rugby Six Nations Championship 2023?
In the reaction $Na_2SO_4(aq) + BaCl_2(aq) \to BaSO_4(s) + 2NaCl(aq)$, what will be the actual physical state that can replace (?)?
Who among the following won the 2024 Nobel Prize in literature?
In 202S, the AGILE Index was introduced to measure which of the following areas globally?
Service and trade fall under which type of economic activities?
Who became the Chief Minister of Delhi in February 202S, marking the Bharatiya Janata Party's return to power in the capital after 27 years?
Which seismic waves are the fastest and travel through both solids and liquids inside the Earth?

options.text: By supporting colonial factories and restricting local enterprises.
By promoting indigenous industries and rejecting foreign products.
By creating diplomatic councils to negotiate tariff concessions.
By adopting military drills to train youth for organised defence.

questions.question_text: How did the Swadeshi movement aim to counter British policies after 1905?
Which schedule contains the assigned functions of the Rural Local Self Government or the Panchayati Raj?
The Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) was enacted in 1999 to replace the earlier FERA law. In which year did FEMA officially come into force?
Which of the following courts is empowered to impose the highest punishment among the subordinate courts in India?

options.text: It mainly builds or elevates parts of the crust
It is controlled by rainfall and temperature
It requires running water to operate

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about volcanism is most accurate in the context of geomorphic processes?
Harshavardhana's sister, Rajyashri, was married to whom among the following?
India follows which model of citizenship under its Constitution?
The Government of India Act, 1935, provided __________.
The 'Make in India' initiative launched by the Government of India in 2014 is primarily focused on which of the following?
The Kadamba dynasty was founded by __________, an expert in guerrilla warfare.

options.text: They can be enforced by any citizen through writ petitions.
They are enforceable by the courts as Fundamental Rights.
They are non-justiciable guidelines that aim to establish a just society.
They are absolute and cannot be amended under any circumstance.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about the Directive Principles of State Policy is correct?
In December 202S, which country officially extended an offer to India for the localised production of the fifth-generation Su-57 stealth fighter jets?

options.text: High birth rates and high death rates
Low birth rates and low death rates
High birth rates and declining death rates
Low birth rates and high death rates

questions.question_text: What is the primary reason for the rapid population growth in developing countries during the second stage of the Demographic Transition Model?

options.text: Shut Down, because it is the only way to achieve zero power consumption.
Hibernate, because it saves the entire system state to the hard disk and powers down completely.
Hybrid Sleep, because it uses both RAM and the hard drive to store the session.
Sleep (Standby), because it maintains the session in RAM for a very quick resume.

questions.question_text: A user is leaving their laptop unattended for the entire weekend and wants to ensure zero battery consumption while still being able to resume their exact work session (all open applications and tabs) quickly when they return on Monday. Which power-saving mode in Windows should they choose?
Pressure is measured in which SI unit?
Which first-of-its-kind propellant-production device did ISRO unveil in February 202S?

options.text: The Chief Minister of the nearest state

questions.question_text: If a new union territory is created, who will appoint its administrator?
Electrical conductors are materials that possess which of the following properties?
Which shortcut saves the current file in Microsoft Word or Microsoft Excel?
Which temple in Odisha is renowned for its chariot-shaped structure and is a prime example of Kalinga architecture?
Aruna Mohanty, who received the Padma Shree award in 2017, is associated with which of the following dance forms?
As per International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) data, which country has the largest installed hydropower capacity in the world as of 202S?
Articles 245 to 255 of Part XI of the Indian Constitution primarily deal with which aspect of Centre-State relations?
In November 202S, which city hosted the Syed Modi India International Badminton Tournament?

A student notices that arithmetic results appear instantly, but loading data from main memory is slower. Which CPU feature primarily reduces this delay by keeping frequently used data closer to the processor?
In October 202S, Max Verstappen won the United States Grand Prix representing which of the following teams?
What is the main objective of India's fiscal policy?
In a certain code language, 'DOLE' is coded as '7348' and 'LEMO' is coded as '2738'. What is the code for 'M' in the given code language?

In the English alphabetical word WORTHLESSLY is changed to the second letter following it in the English alphabetical order AND each consonant is changed to the letter immediately preceding it in the English alphabetical order. How many letters will appear exactly twice in the group of letters thus formed?

Each vowel in the word READING is changed to the letter immediately following it in the English alphabetical order and each consonant is changed to the letter immediately preceding it in the English alphabetical order. Which of the following letters will appear exactly twice in the group of letters thus formed?

Six people A, B, P, Q, C and D are sitting in a straight line. They all are facing towards North. C is sitting to the immediate left of P. Q is a little to the right of the left of B. A sits second to the right of P. What is the position of C with respect to D?
If 2 is added to each odd digit and 1 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 573849, how many digits will appear more than once in the new number thus formed?

Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?
SDH 12 NKR 23 URY 34 BYF 45 ?

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).
Statements:
Some fans are cars.
No car is a bus.
Conclusions:
(I) Some fans are buses.
(II) Some cars are fans.

Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?
MHL21 OJN29 QLP37 SNR45 ?

If '+' means '$\times$', '-' means '$\div$', '$\times$' means '-', '$\div$' means '+', then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?
$32 \times 23 - 14 + 463 \div 232 = ?$

Town G is to the west of Town H. Town I is to the north of Town G. Town J is to the east of Town I. Town K is to the south-east of Town H. Town H is to the north-east of Town K. What is the position of Town G with respect to Town J?

In a certain code language,
'A' means 'A is the son of B',
'A = B' means 'A is the brother of B',
'A * B' means 'A is the sister of B' and
'A ~ B' means 'A is the father of B'.
How is S related to Z if 'S = W * R + T ~ Z'?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

In the following triad, each group of letters is related to the subsequent one following a certain logic. Select, from the given options, the one which follows the same logic.
SIM - LJH, DIME - EDOM
MODE - ODDM

Seven people H, I, J, P, Q, V and Z are sitting in a row facing north. Only J sits to the left of W. Only four people sit between J and K. Only P sits between V and J. K is not an immediate neighbor of Y. How many people sit to the right of Z?

What should come in place of '?' in the given series?
38 55 74 95 118 ?
WT 16 is related to XW -1 in a certain way. In the same way, DE 9 is related to EH -8. To which of the following is NK 19 related, following the same logic?
Tus ranked $50^{th}$ from the bottom and $51^{st}$ from the top in his class. How many students are there in his class?
QQ 5 is related to VR -6 in a certain way. In the same way, LV 12 is related to QW 1. To which of the following is ES 16 related, following the same logic?
In a certain code language, "EUOA" is coded as "53" and "YFMP" is coded as "71". What is the code for "RIZB" in that given language?

What should come in place of '?' in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?
TGA WJD ZMG CPJ ?

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets.
(Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 can be given as 13. Breaking 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
(163, 186, 23)
(101, 100, 96)

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?
(Note: The odd man out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

Refer to the following letter series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only.
(Left) U L Y M R D E L C D A E H R O H F X L D W H (Right)
How many such vowels are there, each of which is immediately preceded by a consonant and also immediately followed by a consonant?

Seven boxes M, N, O, P, X, Y and Z are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Y is kept second from the bottom. Only three boxes are kept between Y and P. X is kept at the topmost position. Only three boxes are kept between O and P. M is kept at one of the positions above O. How many boxes are kept between X and M?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that DOES NOT belong to that group?
(Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)

What should come in place of '?' in the given series?
90 95 126 190 315 ?

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assume that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts. You have to decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.
Statements:
All routers are dongles. All routers are phones
Conclusion (I) - All phones are routers
Conclusion (II) - Some dongles are phones

In a certain code language,
A + B means 'A is the wife of B',
A - B means 'A is the daughter of B',
A % B means 'A is the mother of B' and
A $\times$ B means 'A is the father of B'.
Based on the above, how is C related to M if 'C $\times$ D + E $\times$ F + L $\times$ M'?

Seven boxes B, C, D, E, Q, R and S are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only one box is kept above D. Only two boxes are kept between R and S. Only three boxes are kept between D and E. Only Q is kept below C. B is not kept immediately above S. How many boxes are kept between B and Q?
If 2 is added to each even digit and 1 is subtracted from each odd digit in the number 4314578, then how many digits will appear more than once in the new number thus formed?
